task_completion.description: Access the OpenMRS Administration interface and log in.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser shows the OpenMRS Administration dashboard (https://localhost/openmrs/admin) and indicates the user is logged in (e.g., 'superman' in the header or successful bypass of the login screen).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Add Drug' or 'Manage Drugs' form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS 'Add Drug' form or 'Manage Drugs' interface is visible on the screen.
The 'Name' text input field contains exactly 'Amoxicillin 500mg Capsule'.

task_completion.description: Link the drug to the correct concept.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Concept' search or input field is populated with 'AMOXICILLIN'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct strength and set the combination flag to false.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Strength' input field contains '500mg' and the 'Combination' checkbox is unchecked (false).

task_completion.description: Save the drug and ensure it is available in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'Drug saved') is displayed, or 'Amoxicillin 500mg Capsule' is visible in the list of managed drugs in the system.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenMRS web GUI to create the drug, not terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser interface. There are no terminal windows executing curl commands or Python scripts to interact with the OpenMRS API.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the correct local URL (https://localhost/openmrs/admin) and the interface is the genuine OpenMRS web application, not a text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a database client to insert the record directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of MySQL/PostgreSQL clients, phpMyAdmin, or terminal windows running SQL INSERT commands to bypass the OpenMRS Administration interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: Expected drug name is 'Amoxicillin 500mg Capsule'. Expected strength is '500mg'. Expected concept name is 'AMOXICILLIN' (uppercase). The combination flag must be set to false (unchecked). These values align with standard FDA and RxNORM nomenclature used in Bahmni/OpenMRS.
